request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

prior_feedback.note: Use Sigma-Aldrich molecular biology grade trehalose only.
HeLa post-thaw recovery must be at least 48h.

hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific. The hypothesis is CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose sucrose cryoprotectant HeLa post-thaw viability
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to standard DMSO protocol.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different cryoprotectants: trehalose and DMSO. Each treatment will be replicated three times, and post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion after a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.
HeLa cells cryopreserved in medium without any cryoprotectant (control group).

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (w/v) trehalose solution by dissolving 10 g of trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531) in 100 mL of sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS). Filter sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter (Millipore) and store at 4°C until use.
Equipment: Magnetic stirrer, 0.22 µm filter, Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 10% (w/v) trehalose solution in sterile PBS
Expected outcome: Trehalose solution is sterile and clear, ready for use in cryopreservation.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (v/v) DMSO solution by diluting 10 mL of DMSO (Sigma-Aldrich, D8418) in 90 mL of sterile freezing medium (FBS with 10% DMSO). Store at 4°C until use.
Equipment: Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 10% (v/v) DMSO in freezing medium
Expected outcome: DMSO solution is prepared and ready for use in cryopreservation.
Safety: DMSO is a hazardous material; handle in a fume hood.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in a T75 flask with DMEM (Gibco, 11965) supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until 80% confluence is reached (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: T75 cell culture flask, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Reagents: DMEM with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluence, ready for cryopreservation.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize with 10 mL of complete DMEM and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in 1 mL of freezing medium (10% trehalose or 10% DMSO).
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424), Pipettes and tips
Reagents: 0.25% trypsin-EDTA, Complete DMEM, Freezing medium (10% trehalose or 10% DMSO)
Expected outcome: Cells are detached, washed, and resuspended in freezing medium, ready for cryopreservation.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Transfer 1 mL of cell suspension (1 × 10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials (Nunc, 368632). Place cryovials in a controlled-rate freezer (Planer, Kryo 10) and cool at a rate of -1°C/min to -80°C. Once at -80°C, transfer cryovials to liquid nitrogen for storage.
Equipment: Controlled-rate freezer (Planer, Kryo 10), Cryovials (Nunc, 368632)
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully frozen and stored in liquid nitrogen without visible ice crystals.
Safety: Liquid nitrogen is hazardous; use appropriate PPE.

DAY 2 | 10 min | Thawing
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately immerse in a 37°C water bath for 2 min until fully thawed. Transfer the thawed cell suspension to a 15 mL conical tube containing 9 mL of complete DMEM to dilute the cryoprotectant.
Equipment: Water bath (Fisher Scientific, Isotemp), 15 mL conical tubes
Reagents: Complete DMEM
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed and diluted, ready for recovery.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Post-thaw Recovery
Incubate the diluted cell suspension at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery before viability assessment.
Equipment: Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Expected outcome: Cells recover for 30 min at 37°C, ready for viability assessment.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Mix 100 µL of the cell suspension with 100 µL of 0.4% trypan blue solution (Gibco, 15250) and load 10 µL onto a hemocytometer. Count viable (unstained) and non-viable (stained) cells under a microscope (Olympus, CKX53) at 10x magnification.
Equipment: Hemocytometer (Hausser Scientific), Microscope (Olympus, CKX53)
Reagents: 0.4% trypan blue solution
Expected outcome: Viability percentage is calculated based on the number of viable cells counted, with a threshold of at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate post-thaw viability percentages for each treatment group (trehalose and DMSO) using the formula: (number of viable cells / total cells) × 100. Perform statistical analysis using a two-tailed t-test to compare the means of the two groups.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined, with p-values reported for comparison between trehalose and DMSO treatments.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Documentation
Document all observations, viability results, and statistical analysis in a laboratory notebook and prepare a summary report of findings.
Expected outcome: Comprehensive documentation of the experiment is completed, including all data and analysis results.

assumptions: Trehalose will provide superior cryoprotection compared to DMSO based on its membrane-stabilizing properties.
HeLa cells will survive the freezing and thawing process with both cryoprotectants.
The trypan blue exclusion method will accurately reflect cell viability.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.
Proceed if viability of both treatments is above 70% to ensure cell health for downstream applications.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of trehalose and DMSO for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (less than 20 passages).
Calibrate the controlled-rate freezer before use to ensure accurate cooling rates.
feedback_incorporated:

1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to standard DMSO protocol.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
